response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a long and varied software career, but he fails to meet two of the most critical requirements for this role. First and most decisively, he has no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — the job lists native-level Japanese as a hard requirement, and his listed languages are English, Romanian, and German. Second, while C appears in his skills list and he briefly used C/C++ at National Instruments (a 6-month stint in 2014–2015), his career trajectory is firmly in web/full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), with no demonstrated experience in embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, Linux kernel modification, or the security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) central to this role.
strengths: Long, continuous software engineering career (17+ years) demonstrating adaptability across multiple languages and domains
Some embedded and low-latency systems exposure — hardware work at Altom, low-latency async I/O at Dunnhumby, and C/C++ at National Instruments
Demonstrated ability to work across full stack and pick up new tools quickly, including Linux-based environments
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — this is a non-negotiable hard requirement and is entirely absent from the resume
No relevant security domain knowledge: zero mention of MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, device attack/tamper detection, or encryption protocols
No embedded networking or LAN-switch experience; C usage is minimal and years out of date, with no Linux kernel source-level work

key_factors.factor: Broad engineering background with some embedded exposure
